event: Mesolithic hunter-gatherers settle the island of Britain while it remains connected to continental Europe.
Rising sea levels flood Doggerland, separating Britain from the European mainland and forming the island.
Neolithic farmers arrive, introducing agriculture, pottery, and communal monument building.
The main sarsen stone circle of Stonehenge is erected on Salisbury Plain.
Celtic culture and ironworking spread across Britain during the Iron Age.
Julius Caesar leads the first Roman expedition into Britain.
Emperor Claudius launches the Roman conquest of Britain, founding the province of Britannia.
Queen Boudica of the Iceni leads a major uprising against Roman rule.
Construction begins on Hadrian's Wall, marking the northern frontier of Roman Britain.
Roman administration withdraws from Britain, ending nearly four centuries of Roman rule.
Augustine of Canterbury arrives in Kent, beginning the Christianization of Anglo-Saxon England.
Viking raiders sack the monastery of Lindisfarne, opening the age of Norse invasions.
Alfred the Great becomes King of Wessex and resists Viking expansion.
Athelstan unifies the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms to become the first King of England.
William the Conqueror defeats Harold at the Battle of Hastings, beginning Norman rule.
The Domesday Book is compiled as a comprehensive survey of England's lands and wealth.
King John seals Magna Carta, limiting royal power and influencing constitutional law.
Simon de Montfort summons an early parliament including commoners, a precursor to the House of Commons.
Edward I conquers Wales and incorporates it under English rule.
The Black Death reaches England, killing a large share of the population.
Henry V wins the Battle of Agincourt during the Hundred Years' War with France.
The Wars of the Roses erupt between the rival houses of Lancaster and York.
Henry Tudor wins the Battle of Bosworth, founding the Tudor dynasty as Henry VII.
Henry VIII's Act of Supremacy breaks with Rome and establishes the Church of England.
England defeats the Spanish Armada, securing its independence and naval prestige.
James VI of Scotland accedes to the English throne, uniting the crowns under one monarch.
English settlers found Jamestown in Virginia, beginning Britain's overseas empire.
The English Civil War breaks out between Royalists and Parliamentarians.
King Charles I is executed and England is declared a republic under Oliver Cromwell.
The monarchy is restored with the return of Charles II to the throne.
The Glorious Revolution deposes James II and establishes constitutional monarchy.
The Acts of Union join England and Scotland to form the Kingdom of Great Britain.
The Industrial Revolution begins, transforming Britain's economy and society.
The Act of Union merges Great Britain and Ireland into the United Kingdom.
Admiral Nelson defeats the Franco-Spanish fleet at the Battle of Trafalgar.
Britain and its allies defeat Napoleon at the Battle of Waterloo.
Parliament passes the Slavery Abolition Act, ending slavery across most of the British Empire.
Queen Victoria ascends the throne, inaugurating an era of industrial and imperial expansion.
The Great Exhibition in London showcases Britain's industrial and imperial supremacy.
The United Kingdom enters the First World War against the Central Powers.
The Representation of the People Act grants voting rights to women over thirty and most men.
The Irish Free State secedes, leaving Northern Ireland within the United Kingdom.
Britain declares war on Nazi Germany, entering the Second World War.
The Royal Air Force wins the Battle of Britain, repelling German air attacks.
Britain emerges victorious from World War II and becomes a founding member of the United Nations.
Indian independence accelerates the dismantling of the British Empire.
The National Health Service is established, providing universal healthcare free at the point of use.
Elizabeth II accedes to the throne, beginning a record-long reign.
The Suez Crisis exposes the decline of Britain's imperial power.
The United Kingdom joins the European Economic Community, the forerunner of the EU.
Britain defeats Argentina in the Falklands War to retain the South Atlantic islands.
The Good Friday Agreement brings peace to Northern Ireland after decades of conflict.
Devolution creates the Scottish Parliament and the National Assembly for Wales.
A referendum votes for the United Kingdom to leave the European Union, known as Brexit.
The United Kingdom formally leaves the European Union.
Queen Elizabeth II dies and is succeeded by her son, King Charles III.
